step_by_step_thinking:
Clinical Reasoning:
The patient developed generalized peeling of the skin post-treatment with anti-epileptic medications, specifically mentioning valproate given their history of neurocysticercosis. The timing and location of the skin reaction suggest a fixed drug eruption rather than Stevens-Johnson Syndrome or Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis due to less severe systemic involvement and localized pattern without mucosal involvement typically seen in SJS/TEN.
\boxed{A} Based on the detailed information about the development of generalized skin peeling following initiation of anti-epileptic medication like valproate, along with the absence of widespread mucous membrane involvement indicative of Stevens-Johnson Syndrome or Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis, the most likely diagnosis aligns with a fixed drug eruption.
